product.name: Arox T5 Multi
product.familyDescriptorPl: Tkaniny robocze funkcyjne — antystatyczne i hydrofobowe
product.categorySourceGroup: Polyester Woven Fabrics for Raincoats, Jackets and Coveralls
product.titleDescriptor: 75% Polyester 23% Polyurethane 2% Carbon Milky Coated, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, High Visible Waterproof Oxford Fabric
product.specLine: 250 g/m², 75% Polyester 23% Polyurethane 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Waterproof, Breathable, High Visible, 1/1 Plain Woven Fabric
descriptions: 75% Polyester 23% Polyurethane 2% Carbon milky back coated, flame retardant, antistatic, high visible, water repellent, waterproof, breathable oxford fabric.

Arox T5 Multi fabrics are used for outdoor jackets, trousers and bodywarmers which has waterproof and flame retardant functions. The fabric is inherent flame retardant polyester with antistatic carbon grids.
Certified for EN ISO 20471, EN 343, EN 1149-5, EN 14116 and EN 13034
75% Polyester 23% Polyurethane 2% Carbon Milky Coated, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, High Visible Waterproof Oxford Fabric
250 g/m², 75% Polyester 23% Polyurethane 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Waterproof, Breathable, High Visible, 1/1 Plain Woven Fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 75% Polyester (PES) 23% Polyurethane (PU) 2% Carbon (CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp > 1000N ; Weft > 800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 30N ; Weft > 25N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

matches.rationale: Explicitly certified to EN ISO 20471 and marketed as high-visibility safety jacket fabric.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Antistatic (EN 1149) property present but no explicit statement of electrical utility or high-voltage use.
Inherent FR and chemical resistance present but no explicit welding, foundry, or metal-splash use stated.
Flame retardant certification (EN ISO 14116) present but no explicit firefighter/turnout gear use stated.
